reforms.description: The Law on the Commercialisation and Privatisation of State-Owned Enterprises was promulgated, introducing new rules for the commercialisation of large state-owned enterprises, allowing for commercialisation without subsequent privatisation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the efficiency and competitiveness of state-owned enterprises, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in the management of state-owned enterprises, impacting the broader economic landscape.
reforms.source_quote: In September 1996, and after three years of tempestuous discussions and negotiations... a Law on the Commercialisation and Privatisation of State-Owned Enterprises was promulgated.

reforms.description: Plans were discussed for the creation of new sectoral funds aimed at expediting restructuring and privatisation in heavy industry, which would issue certificates to local inhabitants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative is expected to support local economies and foster innovation in heavy industry through targeted funding.
reforms.status_evidence: Discussions have taken place regarding the establishment of the funds, but no formal implementation has occurred yet.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the initiative is still in the planning phase and does not yet represent a fully implemented reform.
reforms.source_quote: Discussions are also ongoing within and between the relevant ministries on the creation of a new brand of sectoral funds with a view to speed up restructuring and privatisation in heavy industry.
